twitter_thread: 75% of Mac users will be stuck with outdated hardware as Apple skips high-end M6 chips for M7, citing 40% better AI performance as the reason.
M6 Mac chips promised 20% faster CPU and 30% faster GPU, but Apple's M7 shift prioritizes AI workloads, leaving pros with a 10% performance gap.
M7 Macs will get improved Core ML support, allowing for 5x faster image classification and 3x faster natural language processing, but what about raw compute tasks?
Pro users face a choice: stick with outdated Macs or switch to Linux workstations, like Dell's Precision line, which offers 25% better multithreaded performance.
M7 Macs' new GPU architecture will boost graphics performance by 50%, but at a 20% increase in power consumption, straining laptop batteries.
Will M7 Macs' AI edge be enough to keep you loyal, or will you switch to Windows or Linux, what's your experience with ai on mac? #aimac #m7
Developers will need to re-optimize apps for M7's neural engine, a process that took 6 months for Core ML 3, and could take longer this time around.
pack.linkedin: Apple's decision to skip high-end M6 Mac chips for an AI-focused M7 line leaves 75% of Mac users without an upgrade path. With M7 promising 40% better AI performance, developers must weigh the benefits against the 10% performance gap in raw compute tasks. Will the M7 line's AI capabilities keep Mac users loyal, or will they defect to other platforms, and what's your take on this?
tiktok_hooks: Apple just axed the M6 chip, and M7 is all about AI, but what's the real cost?
M7 Macs: 50% faster graphics, but 20% more power-hungry, is it worth it?
Sticking with Mac or jumping ship, what's your move with the M7 line?
pack.reddit_headline: Apple's M7 Mac Line: 40% Better AI at the Cost of 10% Raw Performance, Worth It?
